input.note: Source was already ~1 MP; sent as-is. The native 4 MP woonboot photo lives outside the repo (on user's disk). To re-test at native resolution, drop the 4 MP file at public/test-inputs/woonboot.png and re-run scripts/test-flux2-comparison.mjs — the script auto-downscales >1.2 MP inputs to ~1 MP for cost-controlled comparison, but the chosen model can be re-tested at native res in production.

notes: BFL `cost` field returns CREDITS, not USD. The `cost_credits` field above mirrors that. Approximate USD equivalent at typical BFL pricing (~$0.001/credit): klein-4b ≈ $0.0015, pro ≈ $0.0045, max ≈ $0.01 — total ≈ $0.016 for this run.
All three endpoints (klein-4b, pro-preview, max) accept the same image-editing body schema and returned valid renders. No model needed to be skipped.
